messages.author: nasir
messages.text: Please recommend any easy electives 🙏🏾 It can be any level or from any major.

messages.author: Sofia Papa
messages.text: ARH 131 - Survey of Western Art. It’s super chill. Three exams, take home + online, noncumulative. Weekly 2-4 5-15min YouTube videos to summarize in 125 words is the only work + 1 short project. Workload isn’t bad at all. Attendance is taken but I actually enjoy the lectures and breeze through exams by going to class and listening. I think it helps that I like history but never knew anything about art throughout history so it’s interesting to see. KIN202 - Applied Nutrition for Health and Performance. If you’re interested in preventative medicine/nutrition take Wesley Smith. I personally enjoy his lectures and learn a lot about nutrition for our health. It’s a basic level class, no chemistry or biology required. Quizzes are take home and online. A few in class assignments here and there randomly for attendance purposes - no attendance taken otherwise. 3 exams, no final, third exam is noncumulative during finals week online. No projects.

thread: BUS201 - Money. It’s essentially a personal finance class with more information on taxes and insurance than I learned in high school and honestly didn’t know anything about before this class. Professor Manuel Sicre wants you to come to class and learn and ask plenty of questions. I enjoy his lectures and he engages in conversation a lot, there’s no stupid questions. Attendance is taken every class. Going to class practically guarantees you a 90% and above on every exam. 2 exams (noncumulative) 30 MCQs each. A cumulative 55 MCQ exam for the final. There’s chapter homework, 10 MCQ questions, due before each exam on whatever chapters the exam is on. To prepare for exams he creates practice tests we do in class and can ask questions and review and also a study guide. Professor Sicre actually wants you to learn since he is teaching you about real life things, if you aren’t going to go to class it’s not worth taking.

messages.author: nasir
messages.text: Thank you so much, this is super helpful!!😁

messages.author: Sofia Papa

messages.author: Rachel Farinas
messages.text: Yes, I took her for CIM 458 Immersive Storytelling. I can't speak for other classes but there is no prereq, which lowkey there should be. There might be a chance of you getting put in a team w other people who don't have VR coding experience, which is necessary since your final project is a VR app. The class does not teach coding, only about the storytelling aspect of VR experiences 😅 She will not know how to help you with the technical part and will suggest you speak to Bryson Rudolph in the comm school, who is not always available and works separately in the XR lab. Hope that's helpful!

messages.author: Sebastian Gonzales-Portillo
messages.text: weekly hws but they’re short and easy typically have nothing to do with the quiz, 20 min quizzes every 2 weeks for a total of 6, no exams no cumulative stuff, attendance not mandatory (I’d recommend it tho), lots of math and theory ZERO coding feels, averages will be very low 50-60 range but he grades on a normal distribution so if ur above average you’ll get an A. U can def bs it and get an A but u won’t learn anything unless u put in the effort to understand it. I’d focus on what he says in class and the slide information

messages.text: Has anyone taken this class with Richard Torres or David Spigelman? If so how was it

messages.author: Mia Uy
messages.text: I have heard spigelman sucks like really badly and that was also for an intro microeconomics class

messages.author: Vinny Lam
messages.text: Spiegs for 303 is chill. 320 is not chill Spiegs is very conceptual, so there will be little to no calculations

messages.author: Mia Uy
messages.text: What are the quizzes like in this class and how should I study for it?

messages.author: Nate Joseph
messages.text: Super chill, find/make a quizlet on all the buzzwords he teaches It’s really just a glorified vocab class

messages.author: Mia Uy
messages.text: Amazing thank you

messages.author: Mia Uy
messages.text: Do you have a quizlet i could use?

messages.author: Nate Joseph
messages.text: Q1 https://quizlet.com/test/ece412-quiz-1-study-guide-questions-889928702 Q2 https://quizlet.com/909312125/quiz-ece412-flash-cards/?funnelUUID=fd7be406-7658-4fa1-8e16-246844d8aafe Idk if he covers the same material every sem so make sure it’s accurate

messages.author: Mia Uy
messages.text: Thank you sm nate

messages.author: Miranda Bialek
messages.text: Has anyone taken these “topics and computer signs” classes? The description is quite vague— what is this class? Image

messages.author: Rick Howell
messages.text: All the 'topics in' courses are just special offerings, they're usually fun so if one interests you go ahead and take it

messages.author: Sebastian Gonzales-Portillo
messages.text: 599 at least for me is kind of like a research thing, i basically was able to get credit for doing research with a professor

messages.author: Rick Howell
messages.text: Oh right, 599 is a research one, the others are the one offs
